facts_disclosed: Back to Uber from Lyft — not enough rides on Lyft

turns.content: man i swear this gig economy stuff will humble you real quick
That doesn't sound great. What happened?
so you know i been driving lyft right. bro the last few weeks have been dead. like actually dead
Dead as in barely any ride requests coming in? That's rough, especially heading into what should be a busier season.
wallahi bro i would sit for 30 40 minutes with no pings. in houston. thats not normal. something changed with their algorithm or they got too many drivers or something
That's a huge chunk of dead time. When you're not getting pinged, you're basically paying to sit there with gas and wear on your car. Have other Lyft drivers in Houston been saying the same thing?
yeah thats the thing a few guys in my group chat said same thing. lyft just doesnt have the riders in houston like that. so i went back to uber
Back to Uber — sometimes you just have to go where the demand is. How's it been so far since you switched back?
its been better honestly. at least i get pings. uber takes a bigger cut which is why i left in the first place but whats the point of a better cut if theres no rides
That's the frustrating math of it. A better percentage of nothing is still nothing. At least with Uber you can stay busy and actually bring in consistent money, even if their fees sting.
exactly bro. i dont love uber but they got the market here. gotta be realistic about it. cant afford to sit around waiting for lyft to figure it out
Yeah, you don't have the luxury of waiting for a platform to get its act together. You made the practical call. Do you think you'd ever go back to Lyft if things picked up on their end?
maybe. honestly at this point i just go wherever the money is. no loyalty to any of these apps bro they dont have loyalty to us
Can't argue with that. They'd deactivate you tomorrow without a second thought. Makes sense to treat it the same way — strictly business, go where the rides are.
thats what im saying. like people get attached to one platform and its like bro they dont even know your name. youre a number to them

Exactly. There's no retirement plan, no benefits, no loyalty bonus for sticking around. The whole gig model is built on replaceability. You're smart to match that energy and just go where the money's flowing.
inshallah one day i wont have to deal with any of it. but for now uber pays the bills so thats where im at
That's the right mindset — use the tool that works right now while you're building toward something else. No shame in being pragmatic about it. Uber's just the vehicle, not the destination.
vehicle lol i see what you did there. nah but fr appreciate you listening to me vent about this stuff
Ha, didn't even mean that one. But seriously, anytime. The gig hustle is real and it helps to talk it through. Hope the Uber runs keep coming in steady.
